overview: Zelda Schneurson Mishkovsky was a deeply religious Israeli poet from a distinguished Chabad lineage, known for her humble and mystical perspective. "The Guest" was written as a reflection on Bitachon (radical trust). Zelda felt that the modern world was too full of entitlement and ego; she wrote this to reframe the human condition as that of a temporary, grateful guest in the Creator's home. It is about trading anger and demands for a sense of sacred hospitality and wonder at the simple details of life.
poem_lines: Because I am a guest in this world
I do not want to argue with God.
I want to love the world
as a guest loves a house
where he is staying for a night.
I want to love the light
I want to be a good guest,
